mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as valuation multiples expand alongside fundamental improvements.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding the 2.0% threshold compress equity valuation multiples by raising the risk-free discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as higher borrowing costs weigh on corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the unemployment rate and declines in total nonfarm payrolls signal labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off equity repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as recession fears accelerate.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and robust aggregate supply, which supports corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as supply-side strength offsets demand headwinds.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns do not correlate with rising INDPRO values, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% in a rolling 3-year evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising consumer confidence and credit availability, providing immediate positive momentum to S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as construction and related sectors drive earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with HOUST increases, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points confirms labor market weakening, triggering defensive equity positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as recession probabilities rise.
failure_condition: Sixty-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 55% of instances following the trigger, or the signal's information coefficient falls below 0.02 over a 4-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales and surges in consumer price inflation erode real disposable income and purchasing power, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as margin compression and demand destruction accelerate.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of historical episodes, or the interaction effect reverses sign in a rolling 3-year regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates paired with accelerating consumer price inflation signal persistent monetary tightness, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as real rates climb and equity risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns do not decline when both series rise simultaneously, or the joint signal's predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.01) over any 2-year out-of-sample period.
